introduction: In our continuous endeavor to enhance our operational efficiency, we will be reviewing the volume of plant maintenance work orders that have been closed. This will provide insights into our maintenance schedules and plant downtime management.

subsections.heading: Overview of Maintenance Work Orders
subsections.content:
• Maintenance work orders are primarily generated through our computerized maintenance management system (CMMS)
• In 2020, Elexion Automotive implemented a predictive maintenance model for increased efficiency
• Maintenance work orders are categorized into preventive, corrective, and breakdown types
• The preventive maintenance work orders contribute significantly to minimizing breakdowns
• Our maintenance team consists of 150 staff spanning across all our production facilities
• CMMS has helped to streamline the process and increased the rate of work order closures
subsections.justification: The content provides an overview of the maintenance work order system and processes, and does not answer the DR Question about the number of closed plant maintenance work orders.

subsections.heading: Completed Plant Maintenance Work Orders
subsections.content:
• Work orders are considered completed when all the assigned tasks have been finished and verified
• Maintenance work order completion has a direct impact on the overall plant productivity
• In 2021, the average time to complete a work order was reduced by 15% compared to 2020
• The completion rate of preventive maintenance work orders has significantly improved plant efficiency
• CMMS provides real-time tracking of the work order status, aiding in quicker completion
• We have noticed a decrease in breakdown maintenance work orders due to our predictive maintenance approach
subsections.justification: This slide discusses the completion process and impacts of maintenance work orders, but does not provide the specific number of completed work orders, thereby not answering the DR Question.

subsections.heading: Impact on Production Efficiency
subsections.content:
• Effective completion of maintenance work orders has resulted in a 20% increase in production efficiency
• Our predictive maintenance model has reduced the occurrence of unplanned downtime by 30% in the last year
• An efficient maintenance work order system is crucial for achieving optimal utilization of resources
• The production team and maintenance team collaborate closely to ensure minimal disruption
• The preventive maintenance strategy has significantly reduced the frequency of equipment breakdowns
• With improved maintenance practices, the overall equipment efficiency (OEE) has seen an upward trend
subsections.justification: The content focuses on the impact of maintenance work orders on production efficiency, not the specific number of plant maintenance work orders closed, hence doesn't answer the DR Question.

conclusion: The number of closed maintenance work orders indicates our ability to manage plant downtime effectively. Moving forward, we need to streamline our maintenance planning to further minimize plant downtime and enhance production efficiency.
